violations.text: Establishment is par cooking meats without a variance approval. PIC informed to submit request for variance. New Violation. Correct By: 04/04/2025

An open employees' beverage container was observed stored in the dish machine area next to clean dishes. Employees shall not drink from open beverage containers in order to prevent contamination of their hands, the container, and exposed food. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 03/28/2025

Raw animal foods (eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (mushrooms) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 03/28/2025

Lamb and rice, which were prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Disposed Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/28/2025

Cheese and salmon were observed held at 44-45 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food requiring refrigeration were moved to other units. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/28/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of staff, rice and lamb were placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. New Violation. Correct By: 03/28/2025

Goat was observed thawing on the counter. ROP fish was observed thawing with package intact. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. Goat was placed under running water. ROP fish (under 38 degrees) was opened or (38 degrees or over) disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/28/2025
Containers of fryer oil were observed stored on the floor in the kitchen. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 04/11/2025
Staff were observed polishing clean and sanitized silverware with bare hands. PIC informed; staff obtained gloves. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/28/2025

The floor/wall junctures in the back door area and behind kettle have an accumulation of rodent droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 04/11/2025

Per PIC, establishment had an electrical issue affecting the outlets for the prep units/cold drawers. Units were plugged into other outlets and were maintaining temperatures of 41 degrees F or lower at the time of this inspection. Electrician has been scheduled for service today. New Violation. Correct By: 03/28/2025
The hand-washing sinks in the kitchen (back door and dish was area) and the bar were lacking paper towels. New Violation. Correct By: 01/09/2025
Handwashing sink at the bar is lacking proper signage. New Violation. Correct By: 01/09/2025
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was blocked by a stopper. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/09/2025

The door to the outside, located in the front entrance, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2025
A leak was observed in the at the faucet of the 3-bay sink at the bar. New Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2025
The wall juncture in the dish wash area is in poor repair, observed held with tape. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2025
